steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The confirmed linear factors are $x$, $(x+6)$, $(x-7)$, and $(x-4)$. Any product of two or more of these must also divide $f(x)$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Check each choice by factoring: A = $(x-6)(x+4)$ (roots $6, -4$ — not zeros of $f$); B has negative discriminant so no real roots; C = $x^2 - 11x - 28$ would need roots summing to 11 and multiplying to $-28$, but $7 \cdot 4 = 28$ not $-28$; D = $x(x-7)$ uses two confirmed zeros.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: $x^2 - 7x = x(x-7)$ must be a factor because both $x$ and $(x-7)$ are factors of $f(x)$.

choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — $x^{2}-2x-24$: Factors as $(x-6)(x+4)$; roots 6 and $-4$ are not zeros of $f$. ✗
  • Choice B — $x^{2}-x+42$: Discriminant $= 1 - 168 = -167 \lt 0$; no real roots at all, let alone the given zeros. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{2}-11x-28$: For roots from our zero list to multiply to $-28$ they would need opposite signs, but $7 \cdot (-4)$ is not a zero pair we have. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{2}-7x$: Factors as $x(x-7)$; roots 0 and 7 are both confirmed zeros of $f$. ✓
D is correct.
